file.content:
## The Terrier

Why intent must be stored (the retired "derive from IAM" cinch was self-defeating once the role
prefix leaves the identity name):

1. Without a name prefix, deriving the Terrier from IAM is lossy reverse-mapping — a director set
   is several bindings smeared across project + Mason SA + repo + bucket; partial and ambiguous
   matches become representable.
2. You cannot audit IAM against itself — "intent = whatever IAM says" makes intended-vs-actual a
   tautology that always passes, discarding the drift signal.

Federation forces the same Terrier anyway (a federated principal carries no role marker;
memo-20260527: "the depot remains the home of the roster"), so the Terrier is paid once for both
tiers and makes the routine Terrier read identical and O(1) across tiers.

- **Code vs data.** Capability-set *definitions* are code; the Terrier is *data* (memberships).
  The audit compares: for each (identity, declared-set), are the code-defined bindings present in
  IAM?
- **Physical home (open) — a security boundary, not just storage.** The home must place
  Terrier-write authority at least on par with grant authority (see the audit invariant below).
  This **disqualifies the obvious choice**: a GCS object in the depot's *build* bucket fails the
  invariant, because the director already holds `roles/storage.objectCreator` there (RBSDK) — a
  director could overwrite the Terrier and self-escalate via auto-converge. The Terrier home's
  write-ACL must be reachable by **no capability-set** — held only by governor/payor. Viable
  options: a separate access-restricted bucket/object, or a GAR artifact, each with a
  governor/payor-only write ACL. Decide in-heat. **Cinch: Terrier-write authority is never a
  capability-set member.**
- **Writers (topology-conditional).** The Terrier writer for an entry is the holder of grant
  authority for that entry's capability-sets under the active topology.
  - Federation/multi-admin: the governor writes director/retriever entries; the payor writes the
    governor entry.
  - Keyfile/solo: the payor writes director/retriever entries directly; a demoted or absent
    governor must NOT write entries it lacks grant authority for.

## The audit

content.text:
Found it — and your recollection needs one correction that, happily, makes your argument *stronger* rather than weaker.

## What the documents actually elected

The Terrier's locale is **not** the Manor. Both memos place it depot-side: the citizen-capability memo defines it as "depot-resident data" with the **physical home explicitly open** (`memo-20260605:113-121`), and yesterday's federation canon carries forward "the depot remains the home of the role-to-identity roster" (`memo-20260609-federation-canon.md:120`). No mention of Manor or ecosystem-readable anywhere in the four credential memos.

What you're accurately remembering is the **write-ACL invariant**, which was cinched: *Terrier-write authority is held only by governor/payor and is never a capability-set member* (a director must not be able to overwrite the roster and self-escalate via auto-converge); routine read is tier-blind for everyone. Payor-owned, governor-editable, ecosystem-readable — that shape is canon. The *locale* is what drifted in memory: the named candidates were a restricted bucket or GAR artifact, both depot-side.

## Why your counter-counterpoint survives the correction — and strengthens

The locale was never load-bearing; the **invariant** is. And the invariant is portable to any store with the right ACL — including a Manor-homed object, or for that matter an S3 object in the Cloister. So the real question is what the Depot-less deployment needs from GCP, and the answer is smaller than the Terrier: it needs the **federation issuer**, and that machinery is **org-level and Manor-side, not depot-side** — payor-establish builds the Manor and org; depot-levy is a separate, skippable step. So a **Manor + Cloister** deployment with no Depot gets workforce federation, no resting AWS keys, and a chorister roster homed wherever the write-ACL invariant can be satisfied. Counterpoint 1 doesn't just weaken — it dissolves into tier structure. The canon would want one scoped amendment ("the depot remains the home *of depot-capability rosters*"; a Cloister deployment re-instantiates the invariant in its own locale), not a contradiction.

And here's the structural rhyme that makes me think this was always the right shape: federation canon's **D1** already establishes two permanent identity tiers — keyfile (free, no-org) and federation (paid, org-required) — selected per-deployment by a mode enum. Your Bedrock identity story is *the same enum instantiated across a cloud boundary*: confined credential (bare Cloister, no GCP) vs. federated STS (Manor-coupled Cloister). The consume side doesn't invent an identity model; it inherits D1's.

## Counterpoint 2 — yes, the chorister-as-retriever framing defangs it

You're right, and the existing role grammar already says so: choristers consume images the way retrievers do — a Director somewhere ordained and vouched them; the chorister summons, never builds. Provenance is preserved with **zero foundry footprint per user**. Two honest residuals, neither fatal: a retriever-style image pull still carries a GCP read credential (so "no per-user GCP" becomes "no per-user GCP *authority*, one read-only credential" — or the org distributes images by another channel), and the counterpoint survives **only in the bare tier**, where there is no Director anywhere and kludge is all you have. Which is fine — it parallels D1's free tier accepting resting keys: the bare rung is honest about both of its compromises.

## So the ladder, which I think is your landing place

| Tier | GCP footprint | Identity | Image provenance |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Bare Cloister** | none | resting confined credential | kludge-grade, said out loud |
| **Manor + Cloister** | Manor + org (no Depot) | federated, no resting keys | kludge unless images arrive from a Directored ecosystem |
| **Manor + Depot + Cloister** | full | federated | Director-ordained, vouched, chorister-summoned |

Your lean holds at every rung, and the original counterpoints turn out to be *rung descriptions*, not objections: 1 became the tier enum, 2 became the bare rung's honest label, and 3 (legibility-before-tunnel) stands untouched as sequencing advice. I'm persuaded — and a little pleased that the resolution came from machinery that already existed rather than anything new.

Want me to capture this as the memo's standalone-posture section — the ladder, the D1 rhyme, the chorister-as-retriever provenance answer, and the Terrier locale correction (canonical invariant, portable locale, scoped amendment owed to the canon memo)? The §9/Cloister edits are also still unnotched, so I'd fold it all into one notch.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ
2	
3	## Boundary — shape, not mechanism (cinched)
4	
5	This heat builds the **citizen tier** — the keyfile, no-org operator-credential tier —
6	restructured so its capability layer is identical to the future federation tier's.
7	The backing stays keyfile; a no-org operator runs it unchanged.
8	We do **not** build federation here.
9	OUT of scope: workforce pool/provider setup, STS exchange, IdP device flow,
10	RBRD mode-enum activation,
11	the full drift-audit machinery,
12	and `modifiedGrantsByRole` topology conditioning —
13	all post-MVP (RBSHR "Operator federation"; `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; audit section below).
14	
15	Pre-MVP there is **no compatibility surface**: no depots exist that we must migrate.
16	The grandfather bootstrap (role-from-SA-name recovery) is retired unbuilt.
17	
18	Mechanism lives in `Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md`
19	(this revision supersedes its Terrier-homing, census, mantle, and migration passages — breadcrumb owed)
20	and `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; this paddock holds shape.
21	
22	## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)
23	
24	Canon D2, restated as the bounding premise:
25	**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
26	Therefore the system holds no refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own,
27	ships no unattended-run support,
28	and an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
29	(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
30	This belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` when the civic vocabulary incorporates —
31	it is the constraint that bounds the entire credential design.
32	The unchosen middle credential path — a bash-managed per-operator OAuth refresh token —
33	is recorded as declined under this premise
34	(a durable secret at rest, plus the OAuth-client ceremony multiplied across operators);
35	no current revisit trigger.
36	
37	## The civic structure (cinched this revision)
38	
39	**Payor founds, governors populate, ledgers tell, IAM enforces.**
40	
41	- **Admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.**
42	  The payor's founding gestures: establish manor, levy depot, create the depot's ledger file, seat the first governor.
43	  The payor is outside the citizenry — no ledger entry; its authority is constitutive, not grantable or revocable from inside.
44	  After founding, the payor is ceremonial:
45	  routine administration never exercises the payor credential,
46	  keeping the highest-blast-radius credential cold.
47	- **All citizen administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped**:
48	  enfranchise, expel, grant, revoke, rekey.
49	  A governor may grant the governor capability-set to another citizen of its own depot — governors create governors.
50	  Cross-depot administration does not exist and needs no rule: depot project scoping already forbids it.
51	  Revisit trigger: an org running many depots wanting delegated cross-depot administration gets a manor-scope steward seated by the payor — federation-era.
52	- **Governor keeps its quoin.**
53	  It dissolves structurally: a governor is a citizen holding the governor capability-set;
54	  mantle ceases to exist as a verb.
55	  Singleton-governor is a posture, never a code-enforced constraint.
56	  Standing governor is the default posture for both tiers
57	  (regression-testable administration, cold payor, tier symmetry);
58	  an operator may expel theirs between uses
59	  (known caveat: the on-disk key outlives the revoke by the propagation lag).
60	- **Identity scope is a tier property.**
61	  Keyfile citizens are depot-minted: the governor mints the SA in the depot project.
62	  Federates are IdP-scoped.
63	  A person in two keyfile depots is two citizens with two keys;
64	  the one-identity-across-depots story belongs to federation, where the IdP is the census.
65	  We never build a census.
66	
67	## The Terrier — per-depot ledger files (reshaped this revision)
68	
69	The Terrier is a **collection**: one ledger file per depot, hosted in a Manor bucket, created by the payor at levy.
70	
71	- Write ACL: each depot's governors write exactly their own depot's file, plus the payor.
72	  Ledger-write thereby co-locates with grant authority on the same principal — the write-authority invariant satisfied trivially.
73	- Read population: governors + payor initially (admin-plane);
74	  widen only when a working verb demonstrates need.
75	  Never `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` — citizen names are reconnaissance data.
76	- A rights query ("what does X hold in depot Y") is a pure read of one file.
77	- **The Terrier is admin-plane only — working verbs never touch it.**
78	  Directors and retrievers neither read nor write it;
79	  routine operations go straight to IAM-enforced resources.
80	- Object versioning on: grant history for free.
81	- No global bookkeeping: N independent files, no cross-depot consistency, no "already registered?" lookup.
82	- File format, bucket naming, managed-folder vs per-depot-prefix mechanics: mount-time.
83	
84	## Verbs and orderings (cinched)
85	
86	Civic verbs, all governor-wielded:
87	
88	| Verb | Order | Crash leaves |
89	|---|---|---|
90	| enfranchise | create SA → register in ledger → mint key **last** | keyless SA (sweepable) or registered keyless citizen (rekey completes); never an unregistered key |
91	| grant | write ledger → apply bindings | visible deficit; re-run grant (idempotent) |
92	| revoke | withdraw ledger → remove bindings | visible surplus; report-only, safe |
93	| expel | revoke all held → delete key + SA | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
94	| rekey | new key → deliver → verify → delete old | ledger untouched — keys are not its business |
95	| read-Terrier | pure read | — |
96	
97	Robustness of the verb set against the two open futures
98	(the convergence test: change lands only in named, pre-fenced places):
99	
100	| Verb | Federation arrives | RBRA dropped entirely |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| enfranchise / expel | branch by tier (designed for it) | body swaps, name survives |
103	| grant / revoke | untouched | untouched |
104	| read-Terrier | untouched | untouched |
105	| rekey | untouched (keyfile-side only) | retires |
106	
107	RBRA is **demoted, not deleted**: one credential-kind behind the accessor.
108	Keyfile-tier permanence is itself not promised; the verb set survives either resolution.
109	Capability-set definitions are code, global across depots; memberships are data, local per depot.
110	Grant/revoke operate only on named sets, never raw bindings (contamination guard unchanged).
111	
112	## The audit — MVP is a mirror, not a surgeon (resized this revision)
113	
114	MVP audit: read ledgers, read IAM, print the diff.
115	Report-only — no auto-converge in either direction;
116	the heal for a deficit is re-running the idempotent grant.
117	With nothing auto-converging from the ledger, ledger-write is not an escalation path,
118	which defers the write-authority-vs-grant-authority machinery whole.
119	The full asymmetric-healing doctrine
120	(deficit auto-converge, surplus adjudication, member-first axis, expansion gating, etag concurrency — memo-20260605)
121	remains the cinched future shape, post-MVP.
122	
123	## Ordering — substrate first (cinched)
124	
125	1. **Accessor seam.**
126	   One accessor (likely homed in rba) resolves every token;
127	   no file outside it names an `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` or sources an RBRA file — grep is the gate.
128	   Identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents initially:
129	   call sites keep passing today's role word, reread as a citizen name.
130	   Honors canon D3 at the interface only:
131	   contract silent about mint freshness; no cache built (no consumer yet).
132	   Zero behavior change; suites are the arbiter.
133	2. **Capability-sets as named code.**
134	   Transcribe the binding lists out of the invest/mantle bodies; verbs become thin compositions.
135	   Transcription, not design; zero behavior change.
136	3. **Civic verbs + ledger.**
137	   The behavior-changing movement:
138	   enfranchise/expel/grant/revoke/rekey, ledger files, identity-only SA names,
139	   `RBRS_CITIZEN` station selector,
140	   accessor identity override for multi-identity stations
141	   (the dev/test rig holds several personas; the assay axis already proves the need).
142	4. **Handbook rework to the civic ceremony: home open** —
143	   the stalled handbook heat (₣A6) vs a final movement here; decide once the verbs exist.
144	   Credential handbook tracks stay frozen meanwhile.
145	
146	The substrate movements (1, 2) are no-regret under every model variant discussed;
147	design wobbles cannot reach them.
148	Their correctness is mechanical (suites + grep), not judgment —
149	deliberately robust to agent variance across sessions.
150	
151	## MVP purpose (framing, cinched)
152	
153	The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator with their own GCP billing — one human wearing every hat.
154	The multi-role union citizen
155	(one person, one identity, one rbra.env, all capability-sets)
156	**is** the evaluator onboarding experience; that is this heat's MVP case, not an architectural nicety.
157	Day-after picture:
158	payor establishes manor, levies depot, seats self-citizen as governor;
159	that governor grants director + retriever sets to the same citizen;
160	`RBRS_CITIZEN` brands the station;
161	every tabtarget works through the accessor;
162	read-Terrier prints one line.
163	invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist.
164	
165	## Inherited concern — governor teardown leak (resolved by dissolution)
166	
167	With mantle gone and the governor a citizen under the generic verbs,
168	teardown is expel (revoke-all-first — no tombstone)
169	and replacement is rekey (no delete at all).
170	The standalone fallback (memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak)
171	applies only if the civic verbs are descoped.
172	
173	## Blast radius (cinched, updated)
174	
175	One citizen = one SA = one key carrying the union of held capability-sets **within its depot** — accepted (memo reasoning stands).
176	Depot-minted identity bounds the union at the depot:
177	cross-depot union exposure is federation-tier-shaped (a hijacked live session), never keyfile-shaped (a file at rest).
178	
179	## What done looks like
180	
181	- No call site outside the accessor touches credential files or paths (grep-verifiable).
182	- Capability-sets are named code;
183	  civic verbs operate on (actor, capability-set, citizen) within a depot;
184	  ledger files function as described;
185	  a rights query is a read.
186	- The cult-verb surfaces migrated:
187	  workstation bash, admin verbs, cloud-side naming, the .adoc specs voicing the old verbs, the theurge cases.
188	  Handbook migration per the open home decision.
189	- Suites green at each movement; `complete` before close.
190	
191	## Cinched decisions
192	
193	- Enforcement is server-side IAM; the ledger is intent; no file or client-side check is ever the boundary.
194	- Human-present premise (canon D2) bounds the design;
195	  no refresh tokens anywhere beyond the payor's own;
196	  earmarked for RBS0 as an `axk_premise` voicing.
197	- Payor founds, governors populate, ledgers tell, IAM enforces;
198	  admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.
199	- Governor keeps the quoin;
200	  dissolves to citizen + governor capability-set;
201	  mantle retires;
202	  singleton and standing-governor are postures, not constraints.
203	- Governors create governors within their own depot; cross-depot administration does not exist.
204	- Identity scope is a tier property:
205	  keyfile citizens depot-minted; federates IdP-scoped; no census, ever.
206	- The Terrier is per-depot ledger files in a Manor bucket;
207	  payor creates at levy;
208	  governor-writeable own-depot-only;
209	  admin-plane reads;
210	  working verbs never touch it.
211	- Capability-set definitions global (code); memberships local (data);
212	  grant/revoke only on named sets, never raw bindings.
213	- Intent-first orderings as tabled; key-last enfranchise.
214	- MVP audit is a read-only diff; the asymmetric-healing doctrine is deferred intact, not diluted.
215	- RBRA is demoted to one credential-kind behind the accessor — not deleted; permanence not promised either.
216	- Multi-role is first-class; the solo evaluator's union citizen is the MVP case.
217	- No migration machinery: pre-MVP has no compatibility surface.
218	- The accessor is identity-keyed at the interface from day one; D3's no-per-call-mint assumption honored at the interface only.
219	
220	## Retired cinches
221	
222	- "Roster derives from IAM, not a stored list" — retired earlier (see memo); the Terrier + audit replaced it.
223	  The true invariant (no enforcement state outside IAM) survives.
224	- "Manor hosts the Terrier" as a singular registry — retired this revision for per-depot ledger files
225	  (still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket, but no global census;
226	  both the singular-registry framing and a briefly-explored manor-scoped-citizen-SA model are dropped).
227	- "Keyfile/solo drops or demotes the standing governor" — retired:
228	  standing governor is the default posture;
229	  the true invariant (minimize standing privilege) survives as the posture knob.
230	- "One SA per person" globally — narrowed: one SA per person **per depot**;
231	  the cross-depot one-identity property is federation's, via the IdP.
232	- The memo-20260527 stepping-stone question (role-keyed vs identity-keyed accessor) — resolved:
233	  identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents.
234	
235	## Open — resolve within the heat
236	
237	- Handbook rework home: ₣A6 vs a final movement here.
238	- Ledger reader-population mechanics, file format, managed-folder vs per-depot-prefix layout.
239	- Whether enfranchise+grant get a convenience wrapper for the one-hat citizen.
240	- Whether rekey ships in MVP or immediately after.
241	- RBS0 incorporation timing for the civic quoins (citizen, Terrier, capability-set, the human-present premise).
242	- Breadcrumbs owed:
243	  memo-20260605 (Terrier homing, census, mantle, migration passages superseded by this revision);
244	  the canon's diagram repoint item stands.
245	- `RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC` eviction to RBRD lifetime policy (canon D4/D5) — small, slot it where convenient.
246	
247	## Sources
248	
249	rbk-08-credential-repairs;
250	memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races (the lifecycle split — the enabler);
251	memo-20260527-operator-credential-models (folded into the federation canon);
252	memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak (standalone fallback only);
253	memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model (mechanism, partially superseded as noted);
254	memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process;
255	memo-20260609-federation-canon (D1–D6; the tier boundary and the premise);
256	RBSHR "Operator federation".
257	Prior revisions: 260605 design conversation + two review passes; 260606 Manor/verb dispositions; 260609 morning Terrier naming.
258	This 260609 evening revision folds in a long design conversation (first Fable 5 session):
259	the civic structure and admission scoping,
260	governor dissolution and postures,
261	per-depot ledger Terrier replacing the singular registry,
262	read-only MVP audit,
263	human-present premise propagation,
264	demote-don't-delete RBRA and the verb robustness matrix,
265	substrate-first ordering,
266	and the solo-evaluator MVP framing.
267	Operator commitment: this paddock and its paces are slated by Fable-class agents; density is calibrated accordingly.
268

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣BZ

## Boundary — shape, not mechanism (cinched)

This heat builds the **citizen tier** — the keyfile, no-org operator-credential tier —
restructured so its capability layer is identical to the future federation tier's.
The backing stays keyfile; a no-org operator runs it unchanged.
We do **not** build federation here.
OUT of scope: workforce pool/provider setup, STS exchange, IdP device flow,
RBRD mode-enum activation,
the full drift-audit machinery,
and `modifiedGrantsByRole` topology conditioning —
all post-MVP (RBSHR "Operator federation"; `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; audit section below).

Pre-MVP there is **no compatibility surface**: no depots exist that we must migrate.
The grandfather bootstrap (role-from-SA-name recovery) is retired unbuilt.

Mechanism lives in `Memos/memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model.md`
(this revision supersedes its Terrier-homing, census, mantle, and migration passages — breadcrumb owed)
and `Memos/memo-20260609-federation-canon.md`; this paddock holds shape.

## The premise — human present (cinched; earmarked for RBS0)

Canon D2, restated as the bounding premise:
**a human is present at the kickoff of every run; no run outlives a session cap.**
Therefore the system holds no refresh token anywhere beyond the payor's own,
ships no unattended-run support,
and an orchestrating agent never holds a secret
(device-flow kickoff surfaces only user_code + verification URI).
This belongs in RBS0 as a premise quoin voiced `axk_premise` when the civic vocabulary incorporates —
it is the constraint that bounds the entire credential design.
The unchosen middle credential path — a bash-managed per-operator OAuth refresh token —
is recorded as declined under this premise
(a durable secret at rest, plus the OAuth-client ceremony multiplied across operators);
no current revisit trigger.

## The civic structure (cinched this revision)

**Payor founds, governors populate, ledgers tell, IAM enforces.**

- **Admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.**
  The payor's founding gestures: establish manor, levy depot, create the depot's ledger file, seat the first governor.
  The payor is outside the citizenry — no ledger entry; its authority is constitutive, not grantable or revocable from inside.
  After founding, the payor is ceremonial:
  routine administration never exercises the payor credential,
  keeping the highest-blast-radius credential cold.
- **All citizen administration is governor-wielded and depot-scoped**:
  enfranchise, expel, grant, revoke, rekey.
  A governor may grant the governor capability-set to another citizen of its own depot — governors create governors.
  Cross-depot administration does not exist and needs no rule: depot project scoping already forbids it.
  Revisit trigger: an org running many depots wanting delegated cross-depot administration gets a manor-scope steward seated by the payor — federation-era.
- **Governor keeps its quoin.**
  It dissolves structurally: a governor is a citizen holding the governor capability-set;
  mantle ceases to exist as a verb.
  Singleton-governor is a posture, never a code-enforced constraint.
  Standing governor is the default posture for both tiers
  (regression-testable administration, cold payor, tier symmetry);
  an operator may expel theirs between uses
  (known caveat: the on-disk key outlives the revoke by the propagation lag).
- **Identity scope is a tier property.**
  Keyfile citizens are depot-minted: the governor mints the SA in the depot project.
  Federates are IdP-scoped.
  A person in two keyfile depots is two citizens with two keys;
  the one-identity-across-depots story belongs to federation, where the IdP is the census.
  We never build a census.

## The Terrier — per-depot ledger files (reshaped this revision)

The Terrier is a **collection**: one ledger file per depot, hosted in a Manor bucket, created by the payor at levy.

- Write ACL: each depot's governors write exactly their own depot's file, plus the payor.
  Ledger-write thereby co-locates with grant authority on the same principal — the write-authority invariant satisfied trivially.
- Read population: governors + payor initially (admin-plane);
  widen only when a working verb demonstrates need.
  Never `allUsers`/`allAuthenticatedUsers` — citizen names are reconnaissance data.
- A rights query ("what does X hold in depot Y") is a pure read of one file.
- **The Terrier is admin-plane only — working verbs never touch it.**
  Directors and retrievers neither read nor write it;
  routine operations go straight to IAM-enforced resources.
- Object versioning on: grant history for free.
- No global bookkeeping: N independent files, no cross-depot consistency, no "already registered?" lookup.
- File format, bucket naming, managed-folder vs per-depot-prefix mechanics: mount-time.

## Verbs and orderings (cinched)

Civic verbs, all governor-wielded:

| Verb | Order | Crash leaves |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise | create SA → register in ledger → mint key **last** | keyless SA (sweepable) or registered keyless citizen (rekey completes); never an unregistered key |
| grant | write ledger → apply bindings | visible deficit; re-run grant (idempotent) |
| revoke | withdraw ledger → remove bindings | visible surplus; report-only, safe |
| expel | revoke all held → delete key + SA | partial teardown lands as surplus, never resurrection |
| rekey | new key → deliver → verify → delete old | ledger untouched — keys are not its business |
| read-Terrier | pure read | — |

Robustness of the verb set against the two open futures
(the convergence test: change lands only in named, pre-fenced places):

| Verb | Federation arrives | RBRA dropped entirely |
|---|---|---|
| enfranchise / expel | branch by tier (designed for it) | body swaps, name survives |
| grant / revoke | untouched | untouched |
| read-Terrier | untouched | untouched |
| rekey | untouched (keyfile-side only) | retires |

RBRA is **demoted, not deleted**: one credential-kind behind the accessor.
Keyfile-tier permanence is itself not promised; the verb set survives either resolution.
Capability-set definitions are code, global across depots; memberships are data, local per depot.
Grant/revoke operate only on named sets, never raw bindings (contamination guard unchanged).

## The audit — MVP is a mirror, not a surgeon (resized this revision)

MVP audit: read ledgers, read IAM, print the diff.
Report-only — no auto-converge in either direction;
the heal for a deficit is re-running the idempotent grant.
With nothing auto-converging from the ledger, ledger-write is not an escalation path,
which defers the write-authority-vs-grant-authority machinery whole.
The full asymmetric-healing doctrine
(deficit auto-converge, surplus adjudication, member-first axis, expansion gating, etag concurrency — memo-20260605)
remains the cinched future shape, post-MVP.

## Ordering — substrate first (cinched)

1. **Accessor seam.**
   One accessor (likely homed in rba) resolves every token;
   no file outside it names an `RBDC_*_RBRA_FILE` or sources an RBRA file — grep is the gate.
   Identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents initially:
   call sites keep passing today's role word, reread as a citizen name.
   Honors canon D3 at the interface only:
   contract silent about mint freshness; no cache built (no consumer yet).
   Zero behavior change; suites are the arbiter.
2. **Capability-sets as named code.**
   Transcribe the binding lists out of the invest/mantle bodies; verbs become thin compositions.
   Transcription, not design; zero behavior change.
3. **Civic verbs + ledger.**
   The behavior-changing movement:
   enfranchise/expel/grant/revoke/rekey, ledger files, identity-only SA names,
   `RBRS_CITIZEN` station selector,
   accessor identity override for multi-identity stations
   (the dev/test rig holds several personas; the assay axis already proves the need).
4. **Handbook rework to the civic ceremony: home open** —
   the stalled handbook heat (₣A6) vs a final movement here; decide once the verbs exist.
   Credential handbook tracks stay frozen meanwhile.

The substrate movements (1, 2) are no-regret under every model variant discussed;
design wobbles cannot reach them.
Their correctness is mechanical (suites + grep), not judgment —
deliberately robust to agent variance across sessions.

## MVP purpose (framing, cinched)

The realistic pioneer is the solo evaluator with their own GCP billing — one human wearing every hat.
The multi-role union citizen
(one person, one identity, one rbra.env, all capability-sets)
**is** the evaluator onboarding experience; that is this heat's MVP case, not an architectural nicety.
Day-after picture:
payor establishes manor, levies depot, seats self-citizen as governor;
that governor grants director + retriever sets to the same citizen;
`RBRS_CITIZEN` brands the station;
every tabtarget works through the accessor;
read-Terrier prints one line.
invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist.

## Inherited concern — governor teardown leak (resolved by dissolution)

With mantle gone and the governor a citizen under the generic verbs,
teardown is expel (revoke-all-first — no tombstone)
and replacement is rekey (no delete at all).
The standalone fallback (memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak)
applies only if the civic verbs are descoped.

## Blast radius (cinched, updated)

One citizen = one SA = one key carrying the union of held capability-sets **within its depot** — accepted (memo reasoning stands).
Depot-minted identity bounds the union at the depot:
cross-depot union exposure is federation-tier-shaped (a hijacked live session), never keyfile-shaped (a file at rest).

## What done looks like

- No call site outside the accessor touches credential files or paths (grep-verifiable).
- Capability-sets are named code;
  civic verbs operate on (actor, capability-set, citizen) within a depot;
  ledger files function as described;
  a rights query is a read.
- The cult-verb surfaces migrated:
  workstation bash, admin verbs, cloud-side naming, the .adoc specs voicing the old verbs, the theurge cases.
  Handbook migration per the open home decision.
- Suites green at each movement; `complete` before close.

## Cinched decisions

- Enforcement is server-side IAM; the ledger is intent; no file or client-side check is ever the boundary.
- Human-present premise (canon D2) bounds the design;
  no refresh tokens anywhere beyond the payor's own;
  earmarked for RBS0 as an `axk_premise` voicing.
- Payor founds, governors populate, ledgers tell, IAM enforces;
  admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into.
- Governor keeps the quoin;
  dissolves to citizen + governor capability-set;
  mantle retires;
  singleton and standing-governor are postures, not constraints.
- Governors create governors within their own depot; cross-depot administration does not exist.
- Identity scope is a tier property:
  keyfile citizens depot-minted; federates IdP-scoped; no census, ever.
- The Terrier is per-depot ledger files in a Manor bucket;
  payor creates at levy;
  governor-writeable own-depot-only;
  admin-plane reads;
  working verbs never touch it.
- Capability-set definitions global (code); memberships local (data);
  grant/revoke only on named sets, never raw bindings.
- Intent-first orderings as tabled; key-last enfranchise.
- MVP audit is a read-only diff; the asymmetric-healing doctrine is deferred intact, not diluted.
- RBRA is demoted to one credential-kind behind the accessor — not deleted; permanence not promised either.
- Multi-role is first-class; the solo evaluator's union citizen is the MVP case.
- No migration machinery: pre-MVP has no compatibility surface.
- The accessor is identity-keyed at the interface from day one; D3's no-per-call-mint assumption honored at the interface only.

## Retired cinches

- "Roster derives from IAM, not a stored list" — retired earlier (see memo); the Terrier + audit replaced it.
  The true invariant (no enforcement state outside IAM) survives.
- "Manor hosts the Terrier" as a singular registry — retired this revision for per-depot ledger files
  (still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket, but no global census;
  both the singular-registry framing and a briefly-explored manor-scoped-citizen-SA model are dropped).
- "Keyfile/solo drops or demotes the standing governor" — retired:
  standing governor is the default posture;
  the true invariant (minimize standing privilege) survives as the posture knob.
- "One SA per person" globally — narrowed: one SA per person **per depot**;
  the cross-depot one-identity property is federation's, via the IdP.
- The memo-20260527 stepping-stone question (role-keyed vs identity-keyed accessor) — resolved:
  identity-keyed interface, role-shaped contents.

## Open — resolve within the heat

- Handbook rework home: ₣A6 vs a final movement here.
- Ledger reader-population mechanics, file format, managed-folder vs per-depot-prefix layout.
- Whether enfranchise+grant get a convenience wrapper for the one-hat citizen.
- Whether rekey ships in MVP or immediately after.
- RBS0 incorporation timing for the civic quoins (citizen, Terrier, capability-set, the human-present premise).
- Breadcrumbs owed:
  memo-20260605 (Terrier homing, census, mantle, migration passages superseded by this revision);
  the canon's diagram repoint item stands.
- `RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC` eviction to RBRD lifetime policy (canon D4/D5) — small, slot it where convenient.

## Sources

rbk-08-credential-repairs;
memo-20260604-credential-churn-leak-and-propagation-races (the lifecycle split — the enabler);
memo-20260527-operator-credential-models (folded into the federation canon);
memo-20260605-governor-mantle-tombstone-leak (standalone fallback only);
memo-20260605-citizen-capability-model (mechanism, partially superseded as noted);
memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process;
memo-20260609-federation-canon (D1–D6; the tier boundary and the premise);
RBSHR "Operator federation".
Prior revisions: 260605 design conversation + two review passes; 260606 Manor/verb dispositions; 260609 morning Terrier naming.
This 260609 evening revision folds in a long design conversation (first Fable 5 session):
the civic structure and admission scoping,
governor dissolution and postures,
per-depot ledger Terrier replacing the singular registry,
read-only MVP audit,
human-present premise propagation,
demote-don't-delete RBRA and the verb robustness matrix,
substrate-first ordering,
and the solo-evaluator MVP framing.
Operator commitment: this paddock and its paces are slated by Fable-class agents; density is calibrated accordingly.

content.text:
Your memory wins — and I owe you a clean reversal. The election is real, it's in the **₣BZ paddock's 2026-06-09 evening revision**, and the paddock *explicitly supersedes the memos I quoted*: line 19 says "this revision supersedes its Terrier-homing, census, mantle, and migration passages — breadcrumb owed." I searched the memos and never pulled the paddock — exactly the staleness trap the supersession note warns about. The breadcrumb being owed is *why* my search misled; your "context collision" was you remembering the current shape while I reported the outdated one.

## What is actually cinched (paddock, verbatim shape)

> The Terrier is a **collection**: one ledger file per depot, **hosted in a Manor bucket**, created by the payor at levy.

- **Write ACL**: each depot's governors write exactly their own depot's file, plus the payor — the write-authority invariant satisfied trivially.
- **Read**: admin-plane only (governors + payor initially), widened only when a working verb demonstrates need, **never** `allUsers` — "citizen names are reconnaissance data." This is the one piece of your recollection that drifted: it's *not* ecosystem-readable, deliberately.
- Working verbs never touch it; enforcement is IAM, the ledger is intent; object versioning gives grant history free; **N independent files, no global bookkeeping, no census ever**.
- The retired-cinch list confirms the evolution you remember: "'Manor hosts the Terrier' as a singular registry — retired this revision for per-depot ledger files (*still physically Manor-hosted as a bucket*, but no global census)."

## Wisdom of extending it to the Cloister — my assessment: yes, and it's eerily cheap

The paddock's own principles do the work:

1. **"Admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into"** — the Cloister is simply a *polity whose enforcement plane is AWS IAM instead of GCP IAM*. The payor's founding gesture extends naturally: establish manor, levy depot, *raise Cloister*, create the polity's ledger file, seat its first administrator.
2. **"N independent files, no cross-polity consistency"** — a Cloister ledger file joins the Manor-bucket collection at zero structural cost. That's not an extension being tolerated; it's the design absorbing a new polity kind exactly as built.
3. **The audit ports verbatim**: read Cloister ledger, read AWS IAM, print the diff. Same intent-vs-enforcement spine, second cloud.
4. **Counter-counterpoint confirmed**: the Depot-free middle tier (Manor + Cloister) now has its roster home *already cinched* — the Manor bucket exists at establish, before any levy. Only the bare tier (no GCP at all) needs an exception (local/AWS-homed ledger, or none), and that's tier-honest like everything else on that rung.

One cross-cloud caution, priced honestly: Cloister *admin ceremonies* would write to a GCP bucket — a GCP dependency inside AWS-polity administration. Acceptable because the Terrier is admin-plane only (working verbs — actual Bedrock invocations — never touch it), so a GCP outage can't block inference, only enrollment ceremonies.

## But the homogenization catches two real things in our §12

This is where "fully elect and homogenize" has teeth:

- **§12's verb note is stale against the civic structure.** I wrote "Governor's invest/divest carry over (a precentor invests a chorister)" — but the paddock *retires* invest/divest/mantle/roster entirely ("invest/divest/mantle/roster no longer exist"). Homogenized: a chorister is **enfranchised**, granted, revoked, expelled — the civic verbs, ported. And the precentor follows the governor's dissolution pattern: *keeps its quoin, dissolves structurally* — a precentor is a citizen holding the precentor capability-set.
- **The prebend needs splitting along the code/data seam.** The paddock cinches: capability-set *definitions* are code (global); *memberships* are data (local per polity). My prebend bundled both — model list and agency ceiling are set-definition-shaped (nameable sets like "counsel-quire access"), while the spend allowance is per-chorister *data*. Cleanest resolution: **the prebend is the chorister's ledger entry** — held capability-sets plus allowance figures — making it the Terrier-entry concept wearing its ecclesiastical name. That actually sharpens §9's intersection resolution too: the citizen-side envelope *is a ledger row*.

## Proposed election, for your yes/no

(a) The **Cloister is a polity** in the ₣BZ civic structure; its ledger file joins the Manor-bucket Terrier collection (middle/full tiers; bare tier exception named). (b) **Civic verbs govern the Cloister** — enfranchise/expel/grant/revoke; §12's invest/divest line is corrected. (c) **Precentor = citizen + precentor capability-set; prebend = the chorister's ledger entry** (sets + allowances), honoring code-vs-data. One boundary kept sacred: this is bedrock-memo shaping that *rides* ₣BZ's cinched shape — nothing touches the racing heat's paddock or scope.

If you elect, I'll homogenize §12, fold in the standalone-posture section we deferred (whose middle tier just got materially stronger), and add the federation-canon line 120 staleness to the owed-breadcrumb awareness so the next searcher doesn't repeat my mistake. Elect?

content.content:
152	_Reference_: rbjs_sentry.sh (Phase 3 CIDR rules);
153	the tadmor nameplate's {rbrn_regime} assignment file (current CIDR configuration).
154	
155	**Encrypted tunnel conduit (WireGuard in sentry).**
156	Add WireGuard to the sentry image so the sentry terminates an encrypted tunnel directly to a VPC hosting PrivateLink endpoints for cloud services.
157	The bottle's traffic routes through a `wg0` interface on the sentry — one stable VPC CIDR replaces floating cloud-provider IP ranges.
158	WireGuard is ~400KB on Alpine, uses static keypair exchange (no PKI/CA), and fits naturally into the sentry's existing privileged-container, explicit-routing architecture.
159	New nameplate fields: tunnel mode, peer endpoint, public keys, allowed IPs.
160	VPC side requires a small WireGuard peer instance and PrivateLink endpoints for target services.
161	_Trigger_: when a bottle needs to reach a cloud service that supports PrivateLink (AWS Bedrock is the known first case; survey should include Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, GitLab Dedicated, SageMaker, and others as they emerge).
162	_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (full design exploration, approach comparison, service compatibility survey).
163	
164	**Cloud service conduit nameplate.**
165	A nameplate configuration pattern for bottles that access cloud services at runtime.
166	Near-term: allowlist-only nameplate targeting specific service CIDRs and domains (works today with existing sentry machinery, no development needed).
167	Longer-term: tunnel-mode nameplate using WireGuard conduit (above).
168	AWS Bedrock is the known requirement;
169	the pattern should be researched broadly across cloud AI inference services, managed Git hosting, and other PrivateLink-capable endpoints.
170	_Trigger_: immediate — Bedrock access is a current need.
171	Allowlist-only nameplate can be created now;
172	tunnel-mode nameplate follows WireGuard sentry work.
173	_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (approach A for allowlist-only, approach C for tunnel mode).
174	
175	**Bottle credential custody.**
176	Move the service secrets a bottle workload uses — cloud API keys, IAM keys, SSH keys — off the operator's workstation and into the bottle itself, injected at charge time via nameplate/regime configuration rather than baked into the bottle image (a published image must never carry a live secret).
177	The workstation starts the crucible but never holds the service secret; its only credential becomes permission to charge.
178	This blocks credential theft from a compromised workstation — the one threat the sentry allowlist and the encrypted tunnel do not already close — by shrinking the secret's resting place to the contained bottle.
179	The existing RBRA file regime-path pattern provides the model.
180	Orthogonal to but naturally paired with the encrypted tunnel conduit: the tunnel grants the bottle network reach to a cloud service and the sentry custodies the tunnel key, while this item custodies the service secret inside the bottle — network access and the credential to use it configured together.
181	_Trigger_: alongside the conduit nameplate work.
182	_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (Credential Confinement section; the `Credential theft from workstation` row of the threat-model table).
183	
184	NOTE: For SaaS services without PrivateLink (GitHub.com, GitLab.com), the sentry CIDR/domain allowlist + TLS remains the realistic ceiling.

file.content:
_Reference_: rbjs_sentry.sh (Phase 3 CIDR rules);
the tadmor nameplate's {rbrn_regime} assignment file (current CIDR configuration).

**Encrypted tunnel conduit (WireGuard in sentry).**
Add WireGuard to the sentry image so the sentry terminates an encrypted tunnel directly to a VPC hosting PrivateLink endpoints for cloud services.
The bottle's traffic routes through a `wg0` interface on the sentry — one stable VPC CIDR replaces floating cloud-provider IP ranges.
WireGuard is ~400KB on Alpine, uses static keypair exchange (no PKI/CA), and fits naturally into the sentry's existing privileged-container, explicit-routing architecture.
New nameplate fields: tunnel mode, peer endpoint, public keys, allowed IPs.
VPC side requires a small WireGuard peer instance and PrivateLink endpoints for target services.
_Trigger_: when a bottle needs to reach a cloud service that supports PrivateLink (AWS Bedrock is the known first case; survey should include Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, GitLab Dedicated, SageMaker, and others as they emerge).
_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (full design exploration, approach comparison, service compatibility survey).

**Cloud service conduit nameplate.**
A nameplate configuration pattern for bottles that access cloud services at runtime.
Near-term: allowlist-only nameplate targeting specific service CIDRs and domains (works today with existing sentry machinery, no development needed).
Longer-term: tunnel-mode nameplate using WireGuard conduit (above).
AWS Bedrock is the known requirement;
the pattern should be researched broadly across cloud AI inference services, managed Git hosting, and other PrivateLink-capable endpoints.
_Trigger_: immediate — Bedrock access is a current need.
Allowlist-only nameplate can be created now;
tunnel-mode nameplate follows WireGuard sentry work.
_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (approach A for allowlist-only, approach C for tunnel mode).

**Bottle credential custody.**
Move the service secrets a bottle workload uses — cloud API keys, IAM keys, SSH keys — off the operator's workstation and into the bottle itself, injected at charge time via nameplate/regime configuration rather than baked into the bottle image (a published image must never carry a live secret).
The workstation starts the crucible but never holds the service secret; its only credential becomes permission to charge.
This blocks credential theft from a compromised workstation — the one threat the sentry allowlist and the encrypted tunnel do not already close — by shrinking the secret's resting place to the contained bottle.
The existing RBRA file regime-path pattern provides the model.
Orthogonal to but naturally paired with the encrypted tunnel conduit: the tunnel grants the bottle network reach to a cloud service and the sentry custodies the tunnel key, while this item custodies the service secret inside the bottle — network access and the credential to use it configured together.
_Trigger_: alongside the conduit nameplate work.
_Reference_: `Memos/memo-20260415-crucible-conduit-architecture.md` (Credential Confinement section; the `Credential theft from workstation` row of the threat-model table).

NOTE: For SaaS services without PrivateLink (GitHub.com, GitLab.com), the sentry CIDR/domain allowlist + TLS remains the realistic ceiling.

file.content:
# Citizen Tier — Identity / Capability Mechanics

Date: 2026-06-05

Status: Design captured during the 260605 grooming conversation for heat ₣BZ
(`rbk-14-citizen-model`). The *shape* lives in the ₣BZ paddock; this memo holds the
*mechanism* the paddock points to. Nothing here is built yet. Amended 260605 after two review
passes (a single-reviewer pass, then an ultracode multi-agent pass — see
memo-20260605-citizen-model-ultracode-process.md).

## Scope and vocabulary

This heat builds the **citizen tier** — the current, keyfile, no-org operator-credential
tier — restructured so its capability layer is identical to the future federation tier's, so
that federation plugs in later by swapping identity-kind and token mint and nothing else.

- **Citizen** — a keyfile-tier operator identity: a depot-issued service account holding one
  RBRA key. Rank-neutral (governor, director, retriever are all citizens); the home of the
  identity, not the role. Possession of the key is the identity. (Mints over RBSHR's prose use of
  "citizen" for a depot *artifact* — those are reworded to "holding"; minting rule: semantic
  uniqueness across all persistent names.)
- **Federate** (future, not this heat) — a federation-tier operator identity: an external-IdP
  `principal://` subject the depot recognizes by treaty. A federate holds no key. (This is the
  *Workforce*-federated operator subject; not the envoy's *Workload* "federates-to-AWS" egress,
  which RBSHR Envoy and memo-20260527 Workforce-vs-Workload already separate.)
- **Payor** — the founding human (OAuth refresh token), outside both tiers; neither citizen nor
  federate (memo-20260527).
- **Capability-set** — a named bundle of IAM grants, defined in code (the role's binding list,

All three grep clean across the repo (verified 2026-06-10). The chantry→Quire→endow lineage
was always about governed allowances — a chantry was funded by an endowment — so the prebend
completes a family, not a theme.

**Civic homogenization (elected 2026-06-10).** The ₣BZ civic structure absorbs this hierarchy
whole; nothing here invents role machinery:

- **The Cloister is a polity** — "admission authority is scoped to the polity it admits into"
  generalizes directly; the Cloister is a polity whose enforcement plane is AWS IAM instead of
  GCP IAM. Its ledger file joins the **Manor-bucket Terrier collection** (per the ₣BZ paddock's
  reshaped Terrier: per-polity ledger files, N independent, no global bookkeeping — a new
  polity kind costs one file). Payor founds the Cloister and creates its ledger file; the
  bare tier (no GCP at all, §14) keeps a stated exception — local or Cloister-homed ledger.
  Cross-cloud caution, priced: Cloister *admin ceremonies* write a GCP bucket, but the Terrier
  is admin-plane only — working verbs (actual inference) never touch it, so a GCP outage
  cannot block a chorister's Bedrock calls, only enrollment ceremonies.
- **Civic verbs, not invest/divest.** ₣BZ retires invest/divest/mantle/roster; choristers are
  **enfranchised, granted, revoked, expelled** — the same verb set, second polity kind. The
  AWS credential analog of rekey rides whichever identity tier (§14) the Cloister runs.
- **The precentor follows the governor's dissolution**: keeps its quoin, dissolves
  structurally — a precentor is a citizen holding the precentor capability-set for its
  Cloister. Precentors create precentors within their own Cloister; cross-Cloister
  administration does not exist (account scoping already forbids it).
- **The prebend is the chorister's ledger entry**, honoring the code-vs-data seam:
  capability-set *definitions* (model-list and agency shapes, e.g. "counsel-quire access")
  are code, global; the chorister's *held sets plus allowance figures* are data, local to the
  Cloister's ledger file. This sharpens §9's intersection resolution: the citizen-side
  envelope is, concretely, a ledger row. Enforcement is server-side (AWS) IAM; the ledger is
  intent; no file is ever the boundary — the ₣BZ cinch, ported verbatim.

input.old_string: ## 14. Sources
input.new_string:
## 14. Standalone posture — the deployment ladder (2026-06-10 addendum)

Settled in the same session as §12/§13: the Bedrock consume side **can stand alone without a
Depot**, and the original counterpoints resolved into rung descriptions rather than
objections. The structural key: federation canon **D1** already establishes two permanent
identity tiers (keyfile: free, no-org; federation: paid, org-required) selected by a mode
enum — the Cloister's identity story is *the same enum instantiated across a cloud boundary*,
not a new model. And the federation issuer machinery is **org/Manor-side, not depot-side**
(payor-establish builds Manor + org; depot-levy is separate and skippable), so the middle
rung exists.

| Tier | GCP footprint | Identity | Image provenance | Chorister roster |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Bare Cloister** | none | resting confined credential | kludge-grade, said out loud | local/Cloister-homed (stated Terrier exception) |
| **Manor + Cloister** | Manor + org, no Depot | federated, no resting AWS keys | kludge unless images arrive from a Directored ecosystem | ledger file in the Manor-bucket Terrier |
| **Manor + Depot + Cloister** | full | federated | Director-ordained, vouched, chorister-summoned | ledger file in the Manor-bucket Terrier |

- **Provenance (the chorister-as-retriever answer).** Choristers consume images the way
  retrievers do — a Director somewhere ordained and vouched them; the chorister summons,
  never builds. Provenance is preserved with zero foundry footprint per user. Residuals,
  named: a retriever-style pull carries a GCP read credential (or the org distributes images
  by another channel), and the bare rung — no Director anywhere — accepts kludge-grade
  provenance as its honest label, paralleling D1's free tier accepting resting keys.
- **Legibility before tunnel.** "Users will appreciate the visible WireGuard container" is a
  real fourth force on the Outpost beyond §7's three triggers — visible security earns trust
  invisible iptables don't — but it buys *legibility*, not a privacy boundary the MVP lacks.
  Sequencing: make the allowlist visible first (scry, traffic logs, a "here is everywhere
  your bytes can go" report); build the Outpost when a §7 trigger fires or demand survives
  the legibility fix.
- **Decoupling invariant (cheap now, expensive to retrofit).** Standalone-capable is
  checkable architecture: the Quire/Cloister kindling chain must never source a GCP regime.
  The middle and full tiers touch GCP only in admin-plane ceremonies (ledger writes,
  federation mint), never in the working-verb path.

## 15. Sources

content.text:
Notched — `9c82f47` carries all four files to ₣BZ: the election homogenized into §12, the standalone ladder as §14, the RBSHR breadcrumbs and VPC-SC correction, and the two supersession banners that would have prevented this morning's staleness trap.

## Tie-off inventory

**Already tied, for the record:** instance-vs-menu (answered, question deleted) · Cloister minted and emplaced · civic verbs corrected · prebend-as-ledger-entry · the deployment ladder · SigV4 spike (cinched in §10 as the gate) · probe verb queued with *intone* as register candidate · all owed breadcrumbs landed. The session's chat-only insights all made it into durable text.

**Genuinely untied — two strands, both small:**

1. **The naming-rules statement.** Your parenthetical earlier ("wow maybe we need a statement of these somewhere") is the one idea from this session living nowhere durable. The pieces are scattered: the mint discipline covers prefixes/collisions, but the *no-overloaded-software-words* rule (the `Close` rejection), the register-coherence practice (diplomatic vs ecclesiastical, distinguishable by ear), and the three-register meta-vocabulary (outward: *ubiquitous language* / formal: *quoin, mint, motif* / informal: *cult nouns*) exist only in this chat and one §12 parenthetical. A short subsection in CLAUDE.md's minting section would tie it — but the wording should be yours, so I held off.

2. **A JJK home for the bedrock build.** The memo is shaping, not work-tracking — when the spike day comes there's no itch or heat pointing at it. One line in `jji_itch.md` ("Bedrock Quire MVP — spike gate then allowlist build; shaping complete in memo-20260609-bedrock-quire-shaping.md") would make the formal system aware this work exists. Cheap, and it's exactly what itches are for.

Say the word on either and I'll do it now. Otherwise: the ball really did move — you arrived with a half-remembered memo title, and the memo now holds a settled vocabulary through three registers, a resolved central question, a verified enforcement model, a transport decision with a cheap gate in front of it, a three-rung deployment posture, and full civic integration with the racing identity heat — every claim either grounded in verified AWS behavior or riding a cinch that already existed. Good morning's mulling.
